standards_checked.no-inline-code: pass — these are .py scripts, not command/agent .md files; standard does not apply
standards_checked.no-hardcoded-values: pass — files contain only static workflow step-label strings and stdlib json import; no runtime paths, URLs, thresholds, or environment-specific values requiring parameterization (docstring references in close.py such as 'commands/close.md' are documentation strings, not runtime inputs)
standards_checked.use-source-venv: N/A for .py source files in this scoped audit; standard targets Python invocations in .sh/.md files; no shell or markdown Python invocations present
standards_checked.integer-step-numbering: pass — all steps use sequential integers: close.py labels 1-3, code-review.py 'Step 1'-'Step 6', dev-command.py 'Step 1'-'Step 14', dev-overnight.py 'Step 1'-'Step 21'; deep-search.py uses 'Phase 1'-'Phase 6' which are not Step-numbered labels and do not trigger the decimal-step pattern the standard targets
standards_checked.meaningful-naming: pass — all filenames are descriptive of their workflow purpose; none match forbidden prefix patterns (enhance-*, fast-*, optimize-*, *-v2, *-v3, improved-*, better-*, new-*)
standards_checked.english-only: pass — no Chinese or other non-English characters found
standards_checked.merge-similar-scripts: pass — each file serves a distinct workflow (close, code-review, deep-search, dev-command, dev-overnight) with different step content; structural similarity of the JSON-emitter pattern does not constitute overlapping functionality per Standard 7, which targets scripts doing the same type of work (e.g., multiple validate-*.sh that could share a --mode flag)
standards_checked.concise-documentation: pass — no verbose prose, marketing language, or excessive examples; code-review.py and deep-search.py lack docstrings (absence of documentation is not a verbosity violation)
standards_checked.parameterized-scripts: pass — scripts output static workflow template data that is intentionally constant per workflow; no run-variable or environment-specific values require parameterization
standards_checked.dangling-references: pass — only import is stdlib json; no internal script cross-references
standards_checked.merge-conflicts: pass for the five audited scripts — no conflict markers (<<<<<<, >>>>>>, =======) found in these files; project-wide detector coverage is handled by the combined report

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 2 OBSERVATION_ONLY items (Standard 2 wording precision, Standard 3 N/A vs PASS classification) and 1 MISSED_CRITICAL (Standard 7 merge-similar-scripts on structural pattern similarity) and 1 UNSUPPORTED_PASS (Standard 11 project-wide scope). The MISSED_CRITICAL was evaluated and rejected: Standard 7 targets functional overlap, not structural pattern similarity; merging distinct-workflow todo emitters would obscure caller intent. The UNSUPPORTED_PASS is accurate in scope — partial group audits cover only assigned files, with project-wide coverage handled by the orchestrator's combined report.

findings.issue: Standard 7 may not be clean — all 5 files share duplicated JSON-emitter pattern; 'different workflows' may not satisfy merge-similar-scripts if applied literally
findings.proposed_fix: Flag merge-similar-scripts as critical, or explicitly document a scripts/todo/<workflow>.py exception. Concrete code fix: extract shared todo-emitter logic into a common helper or consolidate into one parameterized generator such as scripts/todo/generate.py <workflow>.
findings.rationale: Standard 7 targets scripts doing the same type of work that could share a --mode flag (e.g., validate-timeout.sh + validate-endpoints.sh). Each todo script emits a distinct, non-interchangeable step list for its workflow. Merging would require a workflow selector parameter, adding complexity while destroying the one-file-per-workflow clarity. The structural pattern similarity (flat list + print) is incidental, not functional overlap.

findings.issue: merge-conflicts PASS is only justified for the 5 audited files; the style-inspector standard mandates running the project-wide detector
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to: 'merge-conflicts: PASS for the five audited scripts only; project-wide detector results are out of scope for this scoped review.' Or run the mandated detector and report project-wide findings separately.
findings.rationale: This is a scoped group partial audit covering only the 5 assigned files. The orchestrator's combined report handles project-wide merge-conflict coverage. Wording updated in standards_checked to clarify scope.

findings.issue: Standard 2 rationale is factually imprecise — close.py contains docstring references like 'agents/style-inspector.md' and 'commands/close.md' but these were described as 'no URLs/paths'
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to: 'Contains no runtime file paths, URLs, thresholds, or environment-specific values requiring parameterization; docstring references and shebangs are not variable runtime inputs.'
findings.rationale: Wording updated in standards_checked to accurately distinguish docstring references from runtime parameterization targets.

findings.issue: use-source-venv should be marked N/A, not PASS, for .py source files; the standard targets Python invocations in .sh/.md
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to: 'N/A for this scoped .py-source audit; no shell/markdown Python invocation is in scope.'
findings.rationale: Classification corrected to N/A in standards_checked.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied OBSERVATION_ONLY wording corrections to Standard 2 rationale (docstring references clarification) and Standard 3 classification (N/A vs PASS). Rejected MISSED_CRITICAL finding on Standard 7 with documented rationale. Logged UNSUPPORTED_PASS on Standard 11 scope with clarifying note in standards_checked.
